title: Boat Rental in Lake Titicaca: Costs, Tips and Things to Do in 2026
topic: things to do by boat in Lake Titicaca
html:
Exploring Lake Titicaca by boat is the finest way to take in its vast, sapphire waters and ancient cultures. Departing from Puno, you can hire a private boat for around €200 per day, and using BoatHire24 you can book a charter online to compare options instantly.

Where to set off in Lake Titicaca

Puno is the main gateway, with a busy harbour called Muelle Turístico where most boat tours depart. You can also launch from Copacabana on the Bolivian shore, home to the picturesque Bahía de Copacabana. Further south, the small town of Yunguyo provides access to the Isla del Sol and Isla de la Luna. The Tiquina Strait is a narrow crossing where ferries shuttle locals and visitors alike. For a quieter start, try the bay at Juliaca though it is less developed for tourism. Each departure point offers a different perspective on this high-altitude lake. You can book a charter online in a few minutes.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small motorboat, Ideal for fast transfers to Isla Taquile or Uros, fits 8 passengers, from €150 for a half-day.
  2. Catamaran, Stable and spacious, perfect for groups of 12 on full-day excursions, from €350.
  3. Sailboat, Great for a leisurely cruise with 6 guests, from €250 per day.
  4. Crewed yacht, For luxury, consider the Maiora 26m for hire, accommodating 10 guests with crew, from €900 per day.

For those seeking a premium experience, the Maiora 26m listing on BoatHire24 offers a fully crewed option with onboard dining and water toys. One option worth a look is the Maiora 26m for hire.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Lake Titicaca?

Rent a small motorboat for a half-day from €150, a catamaran for a full day from €300, and a crewed yacht from €600.

  • Shared group tour (per person): from €25 pp
  • Small motorboat half-day (up to 8 pax): from €150 total
  • Catamaran full-day (up to 12 pax): from €300 total
  • Crewed yacht full-day (up to 10 pax with crew): from €600 total

Prices vary by season and operator, but you can use the boat search on BoatHire24 to filter by price, size, and availability across different providers. You can also see more at check availability.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Lake Titicaca?

No licence is required to charter a crewed boat, but if you plan to drive a private motorboat you may need a valid boating licence from your home country. To see how booking works, visit the boat search.

In Bolivia, a simple permit can be arranged locally for about €20, but always check with the operator as requirements differ between Peru and Bolivia.

High season runs from June to August, when the south-east trade wind known as viento de altiplano can kick up afternoon waves on the main lake. Most operators confirm exact requirements at the time of booking.

At a glance

  • Price band: €25 per person up to €900 per day for a crewed yacht
  • Peak months: June, July, August
  • Trip length: half-day to full-day (4, 8 hours)
  • Guest capacity: 2 to 12 on standard boats
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Lake Titicaca

Can you take a boat from Puno to Copacabana?

Yes, direct crossings take about 4 hours across the lake, covering roughly 60 nautical miles.

What is the best time of year to rent a boat on Lake Titicaca?

The dry season from May to October offers calm waters and clear skies, but July can be windy.

Are children allowed on boat trips in Lake Titicaca?

Most charter boats welcome children, but confirm life vests are provided when booking.

Can I rent a boat for a camping trip on the islands?

Yes, many operators allow you to camp on Isla del Sol or Isla Amantaní if arranged in advance.

To begin planning your Lake Titicaca adventure, check availability on BoatHire24, where you can compare verified listings and even list your own boat to earn extra income.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Lake Titicaca and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
